heroDescription: I build web applications from front to back. Currently at the Legislative Assembly of Goiás, crafting critical systems with Ruby on Rails and modern JavaScript.
aboutHeadline: I build systems from database to browser.
aboutText: I'm a software engineer who cares about building things that actually matter — from database to UI. I've been working in Goiás' public sector since 2021, delivering systems that serve public servants and citizens every day. I believe in clean code, thoughtful APIs, and the idea that well-crafted software saves time for the people who use it.
exp1CompanyFull: Fundação de Amparo à Pesquisa do Estado de Goiás
exp1Desc1: Built scientific management applications in Oracle APEX, automating workflows that researchers and staff previously handled manually.
exp1Desc2: Developed and maintained institutional websites with WordPress, ensuring accessibility, performance, and visual consistency across the foundation.
exp1Desc3: Provided technical support to researchers and collaborators, resolving incidents and managing access with speed and precision.
exp2Company: Assembleia Legislativa do Estado de Goiás
exp2Desc1: Develops and maintains institutional web systems used across the entire legislative body, built with Ruby on Rails and modern frontend technologies.
exp2Desc2: Builds RESTful APIs and integrates institutional data sources into critical, high-availability applications.
exp2Desc3: Modernizes legacy features, reducing inconsistencies and improving the day-to-day experience of public servants.
splitFrontDesc: I build fast, accessible, and enjoyable interfaces — from design to production-ready code.
splitBackDesc: I design and develop APIs, data models, and systems that power the business logic.
